name: hcportal_getKennelHashers
description: Retrieves a list of all hashers (members and followers) for a specified kennel, including their membership status, run counts, payment history from recent events, and credit balances. Requires portal access token validation and appropriate AppAccessFlags (0x40000019 permission check).

parameters.name: publicHasherId
parameters.description: Auth: Public hasher ID of the authenticated user making the request. Validated via HC.CHECK_PORTAL_ACCESS_TOKEN and used for authorization check.

parameters.name: accessToken
parameters.description: Auth: Short-lived cryptographic token validated via HC.CHECK_PORTAL_ACCESS_TOKEN.

parameters.name: publicKennelId
parameters.description: Public kennel ID to retrieve hashers for. Must be exactly 72 bytes (DATALENGTH check).

parameters.name: ipAddress
parameters.description: Client IP address. Logged to LOG.GeneralLog for audit trail.

parameters.name: ipGeoDetails
parameters.description: IP geolocation JSON. Logged to LOG.GeneralLog data field. Falls back to a placeholder JSON if NULL.

rowsets.description: Returned on validation or authorization failures. Uses errorType 2 (validation errors) and errorType 3 (auth errors). Multiple validation errors may return multiple error rowsets due to lack of short-circuit logic.

rowsets.name: KennelHashersList
rowsets.description: Returned on success. Contains one row per hasher who is either a current member or currently following the kennel. Includes profile data, membership/following status, run counts, recent event payment codes, credit balances, and discount information. Ordered by displayName.

columns.name: publicHasherId
columns.description: Hasher's public ID

columns.name: publicKennelId
columns.description: Kennel's public ID (echoed back)

columns.name: hashName
columns.description: Hasher's hash name

columns.name: firstName
columns.description: Hasher's first name

columns.name: lastName
columns.description: Hasher's last name

columns.name: displayName
columns.description: Computed display name from Hasher.DisplayName (computed column that respects NameDisplayPreference)

columns.name: eMail
columns.description: Hasher's email. Shown only if membership is active (MembershipExpirationDate > GETDATE()), otherwise returns '<hidden>'

columns.name: photo
columns.description: Hasher's photo URL. COALESCE to 'bundle://avatar-1' if null

columns.name: lastLoginDateTime
columns.description: Hasher's last login timestamp

columns.name: inviteCode
columns.description: Invite/reset code if hasher has never logged in. Extracted from Hasher.ResetCode by removing 'URC:' prefix. Empty string if hasher has logged in.

columns.name: isHomeKennel
columns.description: 'Yes' if this kennel matches hasher's HomeKennelId, otherwise 'No'

columns.name: isFollowing
columns.description: 'Yes' if HasherKennelMap.Following = 1, otherwise 'No'

columns.name: isMember
columns.description: 'Yes' if MembershipExpirationDate > GETDATE(), otherwise 'No'

columns.name: status
columns.description: 'Member' if membership active, 'Following' if only following (not member), 'None' if neither

columns.name: notifications
columns.description: Notification preference: 'Auto', 'On', 'Off', or 'Unknown'

columns.name: emailAlerts
columns.description: Email alert preference: 'Auto', 'On', 'Off', or 'Unknown'

columns.name: historicHaring
columns.description: Historical haring count (pre-HC). COALESCE to 0.

columns.name: historicTotalRuns
columns.description: Historical total run count (pre-HC). COALESCE to 0.

columns.name: hcHaringCount
columns.description: HC-tracked haring count for this kennel. COALESCE to 0.

columns.name: hcTotalRunCount
columns.description: HC-tracked total run count for this kennel. COALESCE to 0.

columns.name: historicCountsAreEstimates
columns.description: 'Yes' if HistoricalCountIsEstimate = 1, otherwise 'No'

columns.name: dateOfLastRun
columns.description: Date of hasher's most recent run at this kennel

columns.name: membershipExpirationDate
columns.description: Membership expiration date

columns.name: kennelCredit
columns.description: Kennel-specific credit balance from HasherKennelMap

columns.name: discountAmount
columns.description: Discount amount applied to hasher at this kennel

columns.name: discountPercent
columns.description: Discount percentage applied to hasher at this kennel

columns.name: discountDescription
columns.description: Description of discount applied to hasher at this kennel

columns.name: hashCredit
columns.description: Hasher's credit balance for this kennel from KennelCredit.currentBalance. COALESCE to 0.

columns.name: atLastEvent
columns.description: Payment info at last counted run: 'EventNumber-PaymentCode' (e.g., '1234-C' for cash). PaymentCode from Payment.PaymentTypeCode computed column. '?' if no payment found.

columns.name: atSecondToLastEvent
columns.description: Payment info at second-to-last counted run: 'EventNumber-PaymentCode'. '?' if no payment found.

columns.name: secondToLastEventDate
columns.description: Event start date of second-to-last counted run

columns.name: lastEventDate
columns.description: Event start date of last counted run

columns.name: nextEventDate
columns.description: Event start date of next upcoming counted run

columns.name: nextEventNumber
columns.description: Event number of next upcoming counted run (CAST to NVARCHAR)

sideEffects: INSERT into HC.ErrorLog on validation/auth failures (one per failure)
INSERT into LOG.GeneralLog on every call (after validation phase, includes IP data and error status)

codeSmells.detail: SET NOCOUNT ON is present but SET XACT_ABORT ON is missing. Runtime errors will not auto-rollback.
The entire SP has no error handling. If any query fails (e.g., invalid foreign key lookup), the error propagates unhandled to the caller.

Lines 30, 66: Uses DATALENGTH instead of LEN to validate @publicHasherId (checking for 16 bytes = UNIQUEIDENTIFIER) and @publicKennelId (checking for 72 bytes = 36-char GUID in NVARCHAR). DATALENGTH returns byte count (2x char count for NVARCHAR). Should use LEN for character-based validation or validate UNIQUEIDENTIFIER type directly.

Multiple validation blocks run sequentially but only the last one issues RETURN. If multiple validations fail, multiple error rowsets are returned. The @isError flag prevents later validations from running but LOG.GeneralLog insert (line 121) always runs, and RETURN only happens at line 136 after logging.

Line 66: Validates @publicKennelId DATALENGTH != 72 bytes. A UNIQUEIDENTIFIER stored in NVARCHAR(250) as string representation '12345678-1234-...' is 36 characters = 72 bytes. However, this brittle validation would break if trailing spaces were present or if the GUID were uppercase vs lowercase.
Lines 46-63: Validation for @hasherId lookup is commented out. This may be intentional (allowing any valid GUID even if not found in Hasher table), but the commented code suggests it was once active.

codeSmells.issue: Large CTE joins without indexes on Payment.CancelledBy_UserId
codeSmells.detail: Lines 271, 281: LEFT JOIN to Payment with filter 'CancelledBy_UserId is null'. If there's no index on this nullable column, the join could be slow for large Payment tables.

codeSmells.issue: Three separate queries to get last, second-to-last, and next event
codeSmells.detail: Lines 176-201: Three sequential SELECT TOP 1 queries to get last event, second-to-last event, and next event. Could be optimized with a single ROW_NUMBER() query or CROSS APPLY.
Line 151: AppAccessFlags permission check uses hardcoded hex bitmask 0x40000019. No documentation of what this permission represents or how it maps to portal access roles.

Lines 211-224: Complex display name computation is commented out. The SP now relies on Hasher.DisplayName computed column instead. The commented code suggests historical business logic for name display that may be valuable context.

codeSmells.issue: Commented-out JOIN logic for payment data
codeSmells.detail: Lines 256-261: Original logic for joining last/second-to-last event payment data is commented out. The current implementation uses CTEs instead, which is cleaner but the commented code documents the evolution of the query design.

breakingChangeRules: Never remove a column from any rowset
Never change a sentinel value meaning
Never change errorType integer codes without updating all callers
Never change the AppAccessFlags permission bitmask (0x40000019) without coordinating with all callers
